### Descendants

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Oda_Nobunaga&action=edit&section=49 "Edit section: Descendants")]

Nobunaga married Nōhime, the daughter of Saitō Dōsan, in a political marriage, but records indicate that they had no children.
It was his concubines, including Lady Ikoma (commonly known as Kitsuno), Lady Saka, Kyōun'in (Onabe no kata), Yōkan'in, Jitoku'in, Lady Hijikata, and Myōkō'in gave birth to his children.
Kitsuno and Onabe's became Nobunaga's concubines after her husband was killed in battle, and Onabe had two sons with her former husband.
Kitsuno bore Nobunaga's heir Nobutada and his second son Nobukatsu, and Saka bore his third son [Nobutaka](/wiki/Oda_Nobutaka "Oda Nobutaka").
After the death of Nobunaga and Nobutada, Nobukatsu and Nobutaka fought for Nobunaga's succession, and finally Nobutada's son [Hidenobu](/wiki/Oda_Hidenobu "Oda Hidenobu") (Sanpōshi) succeeded the head of the Oda clan.

* **Lawful Wife**: [Sagiyama-dono](/wiki/N%C5%8Dhime "Nōhime") (also known as Nōhime or Kichō), daughter of Saitō Dōsan
* **Concubines:**
  + [Lady Ikoma](/wiki/Kitsuno "Kitsuno") (also known as Kitsuno), daughter of Ikoma Iemune, mother of Oda Nobutada, Nobukatsu and Toku-hime
  + Lady Saka, mother of Oda Nobutaka
  + [Kyōun'in](/wiki/Ky%C5%8Dun%27in "Kyōun'in") ([Onabe no Kata](/wiki/Onabe_no_Kata "Onabe no Kata")), daughter of Takahata Genjūro, mother of Oda Nobuyoshi, Nobutaka and Ofuri, she acted practically as Nobunaga's lawful wife after he moved his base to Azuchi Castle.
  + Yokan'in, mother of Fuyu-hime, Hashiba Hidekatsu and Oda Nobuhide
  + Shunyomyōchō-daishi, mother of Ei-hime
  + Jitoku'in, relative of Takigawa Kazumasa, nanny of Oda Nobutada, birth mother of San-no-maru-dono
  + Lady Hijikata, daughter of Hijikata Katsuhisa, mother of Oda Nobusada
  + Akoko no kata, daughter of Sanjonishi Saneki
  + Myōkyō'in, sister of Ban Naomasa, mother of Nobumasa Oda
  + Otsumaki-dono, sister of Akechi Mitsuhide

  + [Oda Nobutada](/wiki/Oda_Nobutada "Oda Nobutada") (1557–1582) by Kitsuno
  + [Oda Nobukatsu](/wiki/Oda_Nobukatsu "Oda Nobukatsu") (1558–1630) by Kitsuno

  + Fuyuhime (1561–1641), married [Gamō Ujisato](/wiki/Gam%C5%8D_Ujisato "Gamō Ujisato")

### Other relatives

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Oda_Nobunaga&action=edit&section=50 "Edit section: Other relatives")]

One of Nobunaga's younger sisters, Oichi, gave birth to three daughters. These three nieces of Nobunaga became involved with important historical figures. [Chacha](/wiki/Yodo-Dono "Yodo-Dono") (also known as Lady Yodo), the eldest, became the mistress of Toyotomi Hideyoshi. [O-Hatsu](/wiki/Ohatsu "Ohatsu") married [Kyōgoku Takatsugu](/wiki/Ky%C5%8Dgoku_Takatsugu "Kyōgoku Takatsugu"). The youngest, [O-go](/wiki/O-go "O-go"), married the son of Tokugawa Ieyasu, [Tokugawa Hidetada](/wiki/Tokugawa_Hidetada "Tokugawa Hidetada") (the second shogun of the [Tokugawa shogunate](/wiki/Tokugawa_shogunate "Tokugawa shogunate")). O-go's daughter [Senhime](/wiki/Senhime "Senhime") married her cousin Toyotomi Hideyori, Lady Yodo's son.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

Nobunaga's nephew was [Tsuda Nobuzumi](/wiki/Tsuda_Nobuzumi "Tsuda Nobuzumi"), the son of Nobuyuki. Nobuzumi married Akechi Mitsuhide's daughter and was killed after the Honnō-ji coup by Nobunaga's third son, Nobutaka, who suspected him of being involved in the plot.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

### Later descendants

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Oda_Nobunaga&action=edit&section=51 "Edit section: Later descendants")]

Nobunaga's granddaughter Oyu no Kata, by his son Oda Nobuyoshi, married [Tokugawa Tadanaga](/wiki/Tokugawa_Tadanaga "Tokugawa Tadanaga").[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

[Nobunari Oda](/wiki/Nobunari_Oda "Nobunari Oda"), a retired figure skater, claims to be a 17th-generation direct descendant of Nobunaga,[[142]](#cite_note-isu-156)[[143]](#cite_note-nobunari-157) and the ex-monk celebrity Mudō Oda also claims descent from the Sengoku period warlord, but their claims have not been verified.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

### Sword

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Oda_Nobunaga&action=edit&section=55 "Edit section: Sword")]

Heshikiri Hasebe
:   Famous sword by sword smith 'Kunishige'. A Japanese national treasure. 'Heshikiri' means press and cut and is said to derive from an anecdote about Nobunaga pressing and cutting a chabozu who had been rude to him, including the entire cupboard in which he hid.[[145]](#cite_note-diamond181560-159)

Yoshimoto Samonji
:   A trophy from the Battle of Okehazama, which belonged to Imagawa Yoshimoto when he was killed. This sword was owned by Nobunaga, then passed to Hideyoshi and Ieyasu, and was called 'the famous sword that ruled the whole country'.

Yagen Tōshirō
:   Matsunaga Hisahide donated it to Oda Nobunaga. It is said that it was burned down in the Honnō-ji Incident.

Dōjigiri Yasutsuna sword
:   Dōjigiri is one of the [Five Swords under Heaven](/wiki/Five_Swords_under_Heaven "Five Swords under Heaven") (天下五剣) made by Hōki Yasutsuna, this was the legendary sword with which [Minamoto no Yorimitsu](/wiki/Minamoto_no_Yorimitsu "Minamoto no Yorimitsu") killed the boy-faced oni [Shuten-dōji](/wiki/Shuten-d%C5%8Dji "Shuten-dōji") (酒呑童子) living near Mount Oe. It was presented to Oda Nobunaga by the [Ashikaga](/wiki/Ashikaga_clan "Ashikaga clan") family and was subsequently in the possession of [Toyotomi Hideyoshi](/wiki/Toyotomi_Hideyoshi "Toyotomi Hideyoshi") and [Tokugawa Ieyasu](/wiki/Tokugawa_Ieyasu "Tokugawa Ieyasu").

Kotegiri Masamune sword
:   *Kotegiri* means "*kote* cutter". In this case *kote* is a contraction of *yugote* (弓籠手), the [arm guard](/wiki/Bracer "Bracer") used by a samurai archer. This name comes from an episode in which [Asakura Ujikage](/wiki/Asakura_Ujikage "Asakura Ujikage") cut an opposing samurai's *yugote* in the [Battle of Toji](/w/index.php?title=Battle_of_Toji&action=edit&redlink=1 "Battle of Toji (page does not exist)") in [Kyoto](/wiki/Kyoto "Kyoto"). Oda Nobunaga gained possession of this sword and had it shortened to its present length.
